title: Vantage AI pricing explained — Is £5 worth it for AI job prep?

scenes.narration: Vantage AI pricing, explained. Is the five-pound starter pack worth it for AI job preparation?

Quick recap. Free trial — three analyses on signup, no credit card. Starter — five pounds for twenty tokens, one-time, never expire. Pro — twelve pounds a month for sixty tokens. Premium — twenty pounds a month for one hundred and twenty tokens, including the fit score and presentation deck.

visual.heading: The 4 tiers
bullets: Free · 3 analyses on signup, no card

scenes.narration: The token math. One full analysis costs three tokens. Tone rewrites cost one token each. So twenty tokens equals six full applications, or four applications plus a few tone variations. Sixty Pro tokens equals roughly twenty applications a month.
User type one. Casual job hunter. One application a quarter. Use the free trial. The three signup analyses cover you for the year.

User type two. Active job hunter. Six to ten applications a week. Five pounds covers your first week. Worth it because the time saved per application — about two hours of manual prep compressed to ninety seconds — easily justifies the eighty-pence-per-application cost.

User type three. Heavy job hunter. Twenty-plus applications a week, looking for senior roles, doing this for two-plus months. Skip Starter. Pro at twelve pounds a month gives you sixty tokens — twenty applications. Premium at twenty pounds gives you forty applications and includes the fit score plus presentation deck.

Comparing to alternatives. Jobscan is forty-nine ninety-five a month — five times the cost of Vantage Pro. Resume Worded is forty-nine a month. Final Round AI is fifty-eight to one hundred and forty-eight a month. Vantage at five pounds one-time is the cheapest paid entry point in the category.

visual.heading: vs alternatives
bullets: Vantage Starter · £5 once · cheapest entry in category

scenes.narration: Try Vantage free at aimvantage.uk. Three analyses on signup. No credit card required.
